review_comment:
We shouldn't store the entities in a shared container. If we need to communicate with another entity we should use our dispatch helper.

https://github.com/home-assistant/core/blob/dev/homeassistant/helpers/dispatcher.py

It's ok to store the entity ids of the group members.
This integration needs a 3rd party library before we change any code that involves protocol details like this.

Yes, I know I will try to fix this later. I have alot of other changes that I want to do but I'm waiting with those until I have the time to make the 3rd party library.
This is only to fix compatibility with current function, I'm not actually adding anything new.
We're touching protocol code. We don't allow that. The integration needs the 3rd party library first.
I'd recommend to rename the library methods to something more modern, instead of slave/master.
slave/master is the same terminology that bluesound is using in their API. I think the way to go is to match their terms. But if you want me to change it I will of course do so.
It's just a recommendation. If it makes sense or not you need to decide who knows the device.
